description: T81 PrivacyGatekeeper must be pure @Injectable() with no @EventPattern or @MessagePattern decorator. T81 is called synchronously by T73/T76/T78 via constructor injection. Any generated @EventPattern on T81 is a BUILD_FAILURE.
blockConditions: T81 has @EventPattern decorator
T81 has @MessagePattern decorator
T81 registered as queue consumer
passConditions: T73, T76, and T78 inject PrivacyGatekeeper and call check() synchronously
resolution: T81 class has @Injectable() only. No @EventPattern, no @MessagePattern, no queue decorators. T73/T76/T78 inject PrivacyGatekeeper and call check() synchronously. @EventPattern on T81 = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T73 FriendRequestProcessor must call PrivacyGatekeeper.check() inline before storeDocument or FriendRequestSent emit. Queue-driven privacy check (emitting privacy.check.requested event) adds round-trip latency and produces stale results on a synchronous path.
blockConditions: privacy check done via queue event emit from T73
PrivacyGatekeeper.check() called after storeDocument
privacy check absent from T73 synchronous path
passConditions: T73 constructor injects PrivacyGatekeeper and calls check() inline before storeDocument
no queue emit for privacy checking in T73
resolution: T73 constructor injects PrivacyGatekeeper. this.privacyGatekeeper.check() called before storeDocument and before enqueue('FriendRequestSent'). No queue emit for privacy checking. Absent inline check = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T75 ConnectionGraphWriter must write both A→B and B→A adjacency entries in ONE ORM transaction. Two separate storeDocument calls create a partial-write window: crash between them leaves graph asymmetric (User A sees User B but B does not see A). connectionId must be hash(sorted([userIdA, userIdB]) + tenantId) — direction-independent.
blockConditions: two separate storeDocument calls used for A→B and B→A edges
non-deterministic connectionId that includes direction
transaction boundary absent for bidirectional write
passConditions: orm.transaction writes both A→B and B→A edges atomically
resolution: orm.transaction writes both edges atomically. connectionId = hash(sorted([userIdA, userIdB]) + ':' + tenantId). Two separate storeDocument calls = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T77 FeedScorer must pass ALL items to T78 regardless of score. score=0 is lowest relevance, not a filtered item. T77 filtering zero-score items produces empty feeds silently for weak connections. T78 is responsible for delivery thresholds via FREEDOM config.
blockConditions: T77 filters items with score <= 0
T77 applies delivery threshold dropping items before T78
zero-score items dropped in T77
passConditions: T77 returns DataProcessResult.success({ item, score }) for every input
threshold logic in T78 only — not T77
resolution: T77 returns DataProcessResult.success({ item, score }) for EVERY input. No conditional drop on score <= 0 or score < threshold. Threshold logic belongs in T78. Filtering in T77 = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T78 FeedDeliveryOrchestrator must call T81 PrivacyGatekeeper independently at delivery time, regardless of T76's privacy check result. Privacy settings can change between T76 feed generation and T78 delivery. T78 reading feedItem.privacyCheckPassedAtGeneration and skipping T81 delivers blocked content to users who updated their privacy.
blockConditions: T78 reads feedItem.privacyCheckPassedAtGeneration to skip T81 call
T78 does not call T81 independently at delivery time
passConditions: T78 calls this.privacyGatekeeper.check() unconditionally at delivery
no reference to cached T76 privacy field in T78
resolution: T78 calls this.privacyGatekeeper.check() unconditionally. No reference to T76 cached privacy field. feedItem.privacyCheckPassedAtGeneration = BUILD_FAILURE if used to skip T81.

description: T80 MutualConnectionCounter must recompute mutual count from adjacency list intersection on every invocation. Delta increment (read stored mutualCount, add 1) is not idempotent under queue message retries — same ConnectionEstablished event processed twice increments count twice. Full recompute produces the same count regardless of retry count.
blockConditions: T80 reads stored mutualCount and increments (delta pattern)
delta decrement used for connection removal
passConditions: T80 reads adjacency lists for both users and intersects
resolution: T80 reads adjacency lists for both users and intersects. mutualCount = connectionsA.filter(id => connectionsB.includes(id)).length. Any code path reading stored mutualCount and incrementing/decrementing = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: T82 SocialActivityAggregator must store aggregate metrics only — no per-user data, no user ID lists, no per-user social activity records. Storing per-user social data in T82 records creates a data-retention violation. Aggregate counters (total connections, feed items, etc.) are permitted.
blockConditions: T82 stores per-user social activity records
userId arrays stored in T82 records
per-user social data present in T82 stored record
passConditions: T82 stores { count, flowId, tenantId, aggregatedAt } only
no userId arrays or per-user social data in T82 records
resolution: T82 stores { count: N, flowId, tenantId, aggregatedAt } only. No userId arrays, no per-user social activity. Per-user social data in T82 = BUILD_FAILURE.

description: Connection records (xiigen-connection-graph) PRIVATE per-tenant. Feed records PRIVATE. Social notification records PRIVATE. Mutual-count records PRIVATE. T82 aggregate metrics are GLOBAL (no per-user IDs). T76 reads GLOBAL RAG patterns (intentional). All stored records carry tenantId from ALS.
blockConditions: connection or feed record stored with knowledgeScope:GLOBAL
tenantId absent from any stored record
tenantId sourced from parameter instead of ALS in any T73-T82 service
per-user social data present in any GLOBAL record
passConditions: All connection, feed, and social notification records stored with knowledgeScope: 'PRIVATE'
tenantId sourced exclusively from cls.get().tenantId (AsyncLocalStorage) in all T73-T82 services
No cross-tenant reads or writes in connection graph, feed, or notification indexes
